narration: To clone a graph, we walk through the old graph and create matching new nodes.
A map from old node to new node stops us from creating the same clone twice.
decision: Copy once, then reuse that copy if the old node appears again.

predictionPrompt.prompt: Why do we need an old-to-new map while cloning?

choices.label: To reuse the same clone for repeated visits
choices.feedback: Correct. The map keeps one clean matching copy for each old node.

choices.label: To sort the nodes alphabetically
choices.feedback: Not this one. Sorting is not the job here.

narration: That map is especially important in graphs with cycles, where old nodes can show up again and again.
callout: Without the map, the clone work could loop forever.
